title: eremophila lanceolata
intro: Eremophila lanceolata, also known as the Blue Fleabane, is a flowering plant native to Western Australia, belonging to the family Scrophulariaceae. It has been used in traditional Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The herbal preparations of Eremophila lanceolata include teas, extracts, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, but it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
